topic.what: NeuroTaint taint tracking framework for LLM agents with provenance analysis
topic.how: Dynamic Context Provenance Graph backbone + hybrid semantic tracker + sink-driven causal analyzer
topic.why_matters: Traditional taint analysis designed for deterministic program states fails on LLMs where data propagates through probabilistic natural language reasoning with semantic transformations, implicit control influence, and cross-session persistence

characteristics.what_it_measures: Source-to-sink propagation detection F1 score on TaintBench
characteristics.context: Primary benchmark for provenance claims vs generic unsafe-action classification
Distinguishes positive propagation from benign source-sink co-occurrence
Covers explicit, implicit-control, and asynchronous flow classes

characteristics.what_it_measures: Recall on implicit control influence cases via counterfactual analysis
characteristics.context: Causal analysis perturbs source to test counterfactual sink dependency

characteristics.what_it_measures: Recall on semantic explicit evidence cases via embedding similarity
characteristics.context: Covers paraphrased and meaning-preserving rewritten content propagation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Additional offline audit time per execution unit
characteristics.context: Offline auditor not on agent interactive path

good_for: source-sink provenance auditing in LLM agents
detecting indirect prompt injection across semantic transformations
requires: execution logs with tool call sequences

novelty: First provenance-oriented offline auditor for LLM agent information flow
Identifies three orthogonal flow classes: explicit content propagation, implicit control influence, asynchronous provenance reuse
Dynamic Context Provenance Graph persisting lineage across memory boundaries and sessions
Sink-driven causal analyzer using counterfactual reasoning for implicit control detection
TaintBench 400-scenario benchmark across 20 real-world agent frameworks
open_problems: Causal ambiguity cases where implicit control influence not fully resolvable from logs alone
Semantic attenuation in repeated paraphrase or delayed memory rehydration
Precision boundary near topical-overlap and prior-knowledge controls
Scaling to stronger execution models that suppress some attacks before sink is reached
